title: macOS and iOS Support, Security, and Recovery · Support Workbench
summary: A guided support bench extends Apple platform support and recovery record while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of Apple platform support and recovery record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish Apple hardware architecture, macOS and iOS roles, accounts, Apple Account, local users, managed identities, services, keychain, and device-management boundaries.
Use Finder, System Settings, Activity Monitor, Disk Utility, System Information, Terminal, logs, safe mode, recovery, and accessibility tools for bounded evidence.
Trace APFS volumes, snapshots, encryption, free space, iCloud state, local optimization, versions, conflicts, backup, and restore without assuming sync equals backup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Apple Platform Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The workshop variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Apple Platform Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish apple hardware architecture, macos and ios roles, accounts, apple account, local users, managed identities, services, keychain, and device-management boundaries. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Apple Platform Model, keep Apple Platform Model connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The workshop variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Apple Platform Model condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Apple Platform Security — Controls current Apple hardware, system, encryption, app, service, and device-management security boundaries.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Apple Platform Model, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Apple Platform Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Apple Platform Model, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The workshop variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Apple Platform Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: An Apple Account, local Mac account, and managed work identity are the same credential and authority.

options.text: State the Apple Platform Model preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
An Apple Account, local Mac account, and managed work identity are the same credential and authority. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Apple Platform Model.

For commit to the mission model, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Apple Platform Model for Apple platform support and recovery record.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Apple Changed Case structural check is only one gate. Apple platform support and recovery record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Apple Changed Case, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Apple Changed Case precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Apple Changed Case, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The workshop variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.

For CompTIA A+ support Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Apple Changed Case, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A repair that works on one unmanaged Mac transfers unchanged to every managed Apple device.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Apple Changed Case
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Mixed Apple silicon and Intel devices have sync conflicts, trust prompts, full storage, managed policy, encrypted recovery, and peripheral failures. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Apple Changed Case.

stimulus.title: Apple Changed Case failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A repair that works on one unmanaged Mac transfers unchanged to every managed Apple device.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Apple Changed Case for Apple platform support and recovery record.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Apple Changed Case condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Apple Changed Case output and hide the violated Apple platform support and recovery record assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Apple Changed Case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify boot, sleep, power, storage, app, account, peripheral, network, accessibility, backup, recovery, and managed-policy behavior after a least-change repair.
